hypothesis:
HYPOTHESIS: The application of ternary quantization and sparse attention mechanisms can improve the efficiency of large language models in predicting patient outcomes based on medical text data, such as myoclonic status epilepticus prognosis.

PREDICTION: We expect to find a 25% reduction in computational cost and a 15% increase in prediction accuracy when using ternary quantization and sparse attention mechanisms on a dataset of 10,000 patient records.

EXPERIMENT: To test this hypothesis, we will implement a ternary quantized large language model with sparse attention on a dataset of patient records, including text descriptions of myoclonic status epilepticus and treatment outcomes, and compare its performance to a baseline model without these optimizations. The experiment will be conducted on a cloud-based platform with a NVIDIA V100 GPU and 16 GB of memory, using a batch size of 32 and 100 training epochs.

IMPACT: This research matters because it has the potential to enable the development of more efficient and accurate clinical decision support systems, which can help healthcare professionals make better predictions and treatment decisions for patients with complex conditions like myoclonic status epilepticus.
